dig: verb. To move hard-packed earth out of the way, especially downward to make a hole with a shovel.
dight: adjective. Adorned, decorated, or furnished (with); dressed, arrayed, or decked out.
digit: noun. A position in a sequence of numerals representing a place value in a positional number system.
din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
ding: noun. Very minor damage caused by being struck; a small dent or chip.
dint: noun. Especially in by dint of: force, power.
dit: verb. To stop up; block (an opening); close.
gid: noun. A disease caused by coenurosis of the brain, most commonly found in sheep and canids.
gin: noun. A colourless non - aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries;
git: noun. A silly, incompetent, stupid, or annoying person (usually a man).
hiding: verb. present participle and gerund of hide
him: pronoun. A masculine pronoun; he as a grammatical object.
hin: noun. A former Hebrew liquid measure of volume (about 3.8 L).
hind: adjective. Located at the rear (most often said of animals' body parts).
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
mid: adjective. Occupying a middle position; middle.
midi: adjective. Having a hemline at mid-calf length.
midnight: noun. The middle of the night: the sixth temporal hour, equidistant between sunset and sunrise.
might: noun. Power, strength, force, or influence held by a person or group.
mind: noun. The capability for rational thought.
mint: noun. A building or institution where money (originally, only coins) is produced under government licence.
night: noun. The time when the Sun is below the horizon when the sky is dark.
nit: noun. The egg of a louse.
nth: adjective. Occurring at position n in a sequence.
thin: adjective. Having little thickness or extent from one surface to its opposite.
thing: noun. That which is considered to exist as a separate entity, object, quality or concept.
timid: adjective. Lacking in courage or confidence.
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
ting: interjection. Representing a high-pitched sharp sound like a small bell being struck.
dimit: verb. To dismiss, let go, or release.
diting: verb. present participle and gerund of dite
gim: noun. Edible seaweed in Korean cuisine.
ihi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ihievbe.
imi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Anamgura.
ing: noun. A meadow, especially a low meadow near a river; water meadow.
itd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Tidung.
miting: noun. A little one; a mite.
mtd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mualang.
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
tgn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tandaganon.
thd: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kuuk Thaayorre.
thig: verb. To solicit or receive, usually by begging; ask as alms.
thm: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Thavung.
tig: verb. To touch lightly or playfully; to trifle, dally.
tim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Timbe.
tingi: noun. A Brazilian tree, Magonia pubescens, whose seeds yield soap.
tingid: adjective. Of or pertaining to the family Tingidae of lace bugs.
tmh: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tuareg.
